metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the specific link-checker command (tool name + invocation) in AC3 so an executor can run it deterministically without consulting sibling ticket f088.
findings.finding: AC3 references 'the link checker' as the proving command but does not name the specific tool or CLI invocation, leaving an executor agent unable to deterministically reproduce the verification step without guessing.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'verified locally with the link checker'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'verified locally with the link checker' with the concrete command, e.g.: `markdown-link-check docs/README.md` (or whatever tool f088 will use in CI), so the proving command is self-contained in this ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one user-facing validation step to the ACs — e.g. a walkthrough check ('a first-time contributor following the router reaches the correct doc in ≤2 clicks') or an explicit dogfooding note — so structural presence is distinguished from effective navigation.
findings.finding: The ACs validate only structural presence (section exists, table has entries, links resolve) but provide no concrete before/after workflow comparison or dogfooding step that demonstrates the named audiences actually reach the right doc faster — the value-validation mechanism required by F4(d) is absent.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: 'Verify by walking through the router as each of the four named audiences and confirming the linked destination is the correct entry point for that persona.' Or add a fifth AC: 'A dry-run review by a team member unfamiliar with the repo confirms the router lands them on the right page without additional searching.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC3: Since `scripts/check_docs_index.py` (from `f088`) may not exist when this ticket runs, specify the fallback manual link-verification method (e.g., `grep`-based check or prose description of what 'verified locally' means without the script), or add `f088` as a `depends_on`.
findings.finding: AC3 states links are 'verified locally with the link checker,' but `scripts/check_docs_index.py` (the link-checker tool) does not yet exist in the repo — it is created by sibling ticket `f088` which is still `open` and is NOT listed as a `depends_on`. Without `f088` landing first, the executor has no concrete tool to run for AC3 link verification.
findings.suggested_fix: Either add `f088` as a `depends_on` so the link checker exists before this ticket's AC3 is verifiable, or rewrite AC3 as: 'Every router/table link is manually verified to resolve to an existing `docs/*.md` target (e.g. `for f in exit-codes.md local-dev-env.md concurrency.md; do test -f docs/$f && echo OK || echo BROKEN; done`); once f088 lands, the CI gate enforces this automatically.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
